ontology.foodex2_description: Tea, black, infusion, without milk or sugar

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cup of Teh O Kosong as served at a Singapore hawker centre or kopitiam is approximately 200 ml (200 g), containing negligible calories and no added sugar.

nutrient_source.title: Teh O (Black Tea, No Sugar) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Foodo lists Teh O kosong (plain black tea, no sugar) as a distinct entry with the nutrient values used here. All per-100g values are taken directly from this entry.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index not applicable — food contains negligible carbohydrates (0 g per 100 g confirmed by HPB Foodo)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Teh O Kosong contains 0 g carbohydrate per 100 g as confirmed by HPB Foodo Singapore. Because there is no fermentable or digestible carbohydrate substrate, a glycemic index value cannot be meaningfully measured or assigned. The numeric value 0 is used here as a machine-readable sentinel consistent with the validator requirement that glycemic_index be numeric; gi_category is set to not_applicable to convey the true clinical interpretation. GL per 200 g serving = 0.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (2 mg per 100 g); no added sugar or fat
hypertension.guidance: Teh O Kosong is suitable for individuals with hypertension; its sodium content of 2 mg per 100 g is negligible, and moderate consumption (up to 3–4 cups daily) is unlikely to raise blood pressure, though very high caffeine intake should be avoided.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate and zero sugar; no glycaemic impact
type2_diabetes.guidance: Teh O Kosong contains 0 g sugar and 0 g carbohydrate per 100 g, producing no glycaemic response, and black tea polyphenols may modestly improve insulin sensitivity; it is an appropriate beverage choice for Type 2 Diabetes management provided no sugar is added.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero saturated fat, zero cholesterol, negligible sodium; black tea polyphenols support vascular health
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Teh O Kosong contains no saturated fat, no cholesterol, and only 2 mg sodium per 100 g; regular moderate consumption of unsweetened black tea is associated with improved endothelial function and reduced LDL oxidation, making it a heart-healthy beverage choice.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Black tea polyphenols (theaflavins, thearubigins, catechins) with documented anti-inflammatory properties
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Teh O Kosong provides black tea polyphenols with anti-inflammatory properties and no pro-inflammatory nutrients; it is generally suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis when consumed without added sugar, though it does not independently meet the full positive anti-inflammatory criterion threshold.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Teh O Kosong is a traditional Singaporean unsweetened black tea beverage, widely consumed at kopitiams (traditional coffee shops) and hawker centres across Singapore and Malaysia. 'Teh' means tea in Malay, 'O' indicates no milk, and 'Kosong' means empty or zero — signifying no sugar. The drink is prepared by steeping robust black tea leaves (commonly a blend of Ceylon and Assam teas) in boiling water, then served plain without milk or sugar. The result is a clear, amber-coloured infusion with a mildly astringent, slightly bitter flavour profile and a light, watery body. Nutritionally, Teh O Kosong is essentially calorie-free per 100 g, containing 0 kcal energy, 0 g protein, 0 g fat, 0 g carbohydrate, and 0 g sugar, with only 2 mg sodium and 1 mg calcium. It is naturally rich in black tea polyphenols — theaflavins, thearubigins, and catechins — which have documented antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Clinically, it is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes (zero glycaemic impact), hypertension (negligible sodium), and cardiovascular disease (no saturated fat or cholesterol). It is vegan, halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free. Moderate consumption of 2–4 cups per day is considered safe for most adults; excessive intake may cause caffeine-related effects.
common_user_queries: Is Teh O Kosong good for diabetics?
How many calories in Teh O Kosong?
Is plain black tea suitable for hypertension?
What does kosong mean in Teh O Kosong?
Can I drink Teh O Kosong if I have high blood pressure?
What is the difference between Teh O and Teh O Kosong?
Is unsweetened black tea good for weight loss?
Does Teh O Kosong have caffeine?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Singapore HPB Foodo database, which lists Teh O kosong (plain black tea, no sugar) as a distinct named entry with confirmed per-100g values. The food is a well-defined, standardised beverage with no preparation variability in its unsweetened form.
notes: Zero-macro justification: protein_g is naturally zero — plain brewed black tea contains no protein at detectable levels per HPB Foodo. fat_g is naturally zero — plain brewed black tea contains no fat. carbohydrate_g is naturally zero — Teh O Kosong contains no added sugar and the tea solids contribute negligible carbohydrate below detection threshold; confirmed zero per HPB Foodo Singapore. energy_kcal is naturally zero consistent with all macros being zero. iron_mg reported as 0.0 per HPB Foodo (confirmed zero). Negative sentinel values: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid breakdown for plain brewed black tea not reported in HPB Foodo Singapore database as of 2026. Glycemic index: gi_category is set to not_applicable because this food contains 0 g carbohydrate per 100 g and no glycaemic response is possible. The numeric field glycemic_index is set to 0 as a machine-readable sentinel value required by the validator; the true clinical interpretation is that GI is not applicable rather than low. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0. Rheumatoid arthritis suitability from input has been reviewed: the input contained a contradictory reason/caution pair; the health_context entry has been corrected to reflect that black tea polyphenols provide anti-inflammatory benefit and the food is generally suitable with low concern level.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (0.0), sodium_mg (2.0), saturated_fat_g (0.0), sugar_g (0.0) all present as numeric values, consistent with HPB Foodo data.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index is set to numeric value 0 to satisfy the validator requirement that the field be numeric. gi_category is set to not_applicable to convey the correct clinical interpretation that GI cannot be measured for a zero-carbohydrate food. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0. gi_source is documented with full rationale explaining the sentinel value convention. This satisfies both the numeric requirement and the zero-carbohydrate GI rule.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn URI provided for black tea beverage. FoodEx2 code A0EJW for tea black infusion without milk or sugar. SNOMED CT 227313005 for black tea substance. All codes are defensible and consistent with the food identity.

Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. Rheumatoid arthritis included as additional condition.
